heading: Measures to reduce military spending by developing nations
label: 286mm Measures to reduce military spending by developing nations
notes: Change of NameCommittee on Banking, Finance and Urban Affairs of House of Representatives treated as referring to Committee on Banking and Financial Services of House of Representatives by , set out as a note preceding , The Congress. Committee on Banking and Financial Services of House of Representatives abolished and replaced by Committee on Financial Services of House of Representatives, and jurisdiction over matters relating to securities and exchanges and insurance generally transferred from Committee on Energy and Commerce of House of Representatives by House Resolution No. 5, One Hundred Seventh Congress, .section 1(a) of Pub. L. 104–14section 21 of Title 2Jan. 3, 2001

children.heading: Development by Fund of means to measure military spending
children.label: a Development by Fund of means to measure military spending

children.heading: Position of the United States
children.label: 1 Position of the United States
children.text: The United States Executive Director of the Fund shall use the voice and vote of the United States to urge the Fund, in consultation with the Bank, to continue to develop an economic methodology to measure the level of military spending by each developing country.

children.heading: Progress report to the Congress
children.label: 2 Progress report to the Congress
children.text: No later than 1 year after , the Secretary of the Treasury shall submit to the Committee on Banking, Finance and Urban Affairs of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Banking, Housing, and Urban Affairs and the Committee on Foreign Relations of the Senate a report on the status of the development by the Fund of a workable economic methodology to measure military spending by developing countries.October 24, 1992

children.heading: Annual reports by Fund on levels of military spending
children.label: b Annual reports by Fund on levels of military spending
children.text: The United States Executive Director of the Fund shall use the voice and vote of the United States to urge the Fund, beginning with 1994, to provide the Executive Board of the Fund with annual reports stating the estimate by the Fund of the level of military spending by each developing country in the immediately preceding calendar year (or, with respect to developing countries whose fiscal years are not calendar years, in the most recently completed fiscal year of the developing country), not later than the date of the annual fall Interim and Development Committee meetings.

children.heading: Analysis and assessment of military spending to be included in article IV consultations by Fund
children.label: c Analysis and assessment of military spending to be included in article IV consultations by Fund
children.text: The United States Executive Director of the Fund shall use the voice and vote of the United States to urge the Fund, beginning no later than the date of the first report provided as described in subsection (b), to include in every article IV consultation with a developing country an analysis of the level of military spending by the developing country in the immediately preceding calendar year (or, with respect to developing countries whose fiscal years are not calendar years, in the most recently completed fiscal year of the developing country).
